remediation.summary: Disable SSLv2. The protocol has been broken since 1995 and exposes DROWN.
Disable SSLv3. The protocol is vulnerable to POODLE and has no remaining safe use case.
Disable TLS 1.0. The protocol is deprecated (RFC 8996) and its CBC paths are exploitable via BEAST.
Disable TLS 1.1. The protocol is deprecated (RFC 8996) and offers no security benefit over TLS 1.2.
Enable TLS 1.2 alongside TLS 1.3 so legacy clients can still connect.
Enable TLS 1.3 — modern client support is universal and the protocol is faster and safer.

checks.title: Certificate chain does not validate
checks.score_impact: Caps TLS grade at T (no trust)

remediation.summary: Use a certificate signed by a publicly-trusted CA. Free options: Let's Encrypt, ZeroSSL.

checks.title: Certificate expired
checks.score_impact: Caps TLS grade at T (no trust)

remediation.summary: Renew the certificate. Configure automated renewal (certbot, acme.sh) so this never happens again.

checks.title: Self-signed certificate
checks.score_impact: Caps TLS grade at T (no trust)

remediation.summary: Replace the self-signed certificate with one issued by a publicly-trusted CA.

checks.title: Certificate hostname mismatch
checks.score_impact: Caps TLS grade at T (no trust)

remediation.summary: Issue a certificate whose SAN list includes every hostname this server answers under.
Restrict the cipher list to ECDHE/DHE suites so every session uses an ephemeral key exchange.
Restrict to AEAD suites (GCM, ChaCha20-Poly1305). CBC modes are exposed to padding oracles and timing attacks.
Remove 3DES. Its 64-bit block size is exploitable via Sweet32 over long-lived connections.
Remove RC4. The cipher has a known biases-in-keystream attack and is deprecated (RFC 7465).
Remove anonymous suites (DH_anon, ECDH_anon). They provide no authentication.

checks.title: POODLE (CVE-2014-3566)
checks.score_impact: Caps TLS grade at F (via SSLv3 floor)

remediation.summary: Disable SSLv3 — POODLE exploits its CBC padding handling. There is no safe SSLv3 configuration.

checks.title: DROWN (CVE-2016-0800)
checks.score_impact: Caps TLS grade at F (via SSLv2 floor)

remediation.summary: Disable SSLv2 on every service sharing this key, not only HTTPS. DROWN exploits SSLv2 to decrypt TLS sessions that reuse the certificate.

checks.title: BEAST (CVE-2011-3389)
checks.score_impact: Mitigated client-side in modern browsers; server-side fix is to disable TLS 1.0

remediation.summary: Disable TLS 1.0. BEAST targets its CBC IV reuse — TLS 1.1+ uses per-record IVs.

checks.title: Sweet32 (CVE-2016-2183)
checks.score_impact: Caps TLS grade at C (via 3DES floor)

remediation.summary: Remove 3DES — 64-bit block ciphers are vulnerable to birthday-bound attacks over long sessions.

checks.title: RC4 weakness (CVE-2015-2808)
checks.score_impact: Caps TLS grade at F (via RC4 floor)

remediation.summary: Remove RC4 from the cipher list. The keystream biases let an attacker recover plaintext over many sessions.

checks.title: Strict-Transport-Security
checks.score_impact: 20/100 of the Headers score; required for A+

remediation.summary: Emit HSTS with at least one year of max-age, includeSubDomains, and preload to be eligible for the HSTS preload list.

checks.title: Content-Security-Policy
checks.score_impact: 25/100 of the Headers score (the largest single weight)

remediation.summary: Ship a strict CSP without unsafe-inline in script-src. Use nonces or hashes for inline scripts.
remediation.example_snippet: add_header Content-Security-Policy "default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data:; object-src 'none'; base-uri 'self'; frame-ancestors 'none';" always;

remediation.summary: Send X-Frame-Options: DENY or rely on CSP's frame-ancestors directive (modern equivalent).
Send X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff to disable MIME-sniffing on browsers that still perform it.
Send a restrictive Referrer-Policy. "strict-origin-when-cross-origin" is a good default.
Send a Permissions-Policy that explicitly denies powerful APIs you do not use (geolocation, camera, microphone, payment).
remediation.example_snippet: add_header Permissions-Policy "geolocation=(), camera=(), microphone=(), payment=()" always;

remediation.summary: Send COOP: same-origin to isolate top-level browsing contexts and enable cross-origin-isolated features.
Send COEP: require-corp to require explicit opt-in from cross-origin resources.
Send CORP: same-origin (or same-site) so cross-origin contexts cannot read your resources.
Suppress the Server header (or replace its value) so attackers cannot fingerprint the running software.
Set the Secure attribute on every cookie served from HTTPS. Without it the cookie can be sent over plaintext HTTP.
Set SameSite=Lax or SameSite=Strict on every cookie to mitigate CSRF.
remediation.example_snippet: res.cookie('session', token, { secure: true, httpOnly: true, sameSite: 'strict' });

remediation.summary: Replace the wildcard with an explicit allow-list, or remove the header on endpoints that do not need CORS.

checks.title: security.txt
checks.score_impact: Informational; does not affect any grade

remediation.summary: Publish a RFC 9116 security.txt at /.well-known/security.txt. Include Contact, Expires and, ideally, a PGP signature.
remediation.example_snippet:
Contact: mailto:security@example.com
Expires: 2026-12-31T00:00:00Z
Preferred-Languages: en, fr
Canonical: https://example.com/.well-known/security.txt

checks.title: robots.txt
checks.score_impact: Informational; suspicious disallows do not affect any grade but leak internal paths

remediation.summary: Avoid listing sensitive paths in Disallow — robots.txt is publicly readable and effectively advertises which paths to probe.
remediation.example_snippet:
User-agent: *
Disallow: /search
Disallow: /private/  # avoid; protect via auth instead
Sitemap: https://example.com/sitemap.xml
